content: Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language. Its design philosophy emphasises code readability using indentation.
Python supports multiple programming paradigms including structured, object-oriented, and functional programming.
Guido van Rossum began working on Python in the late 1980s as a successor to the ABC programming language.
Python 3.0 was released in 2008 and introduced breaking changes that were not backward-compatible with Python 2.
The Python Package Index (PyPI) hosts thousands of third-party packages that extend the Python standard library.
